## {#star-formation background-color="#533860"}

::: {style="color: white; font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: bold;"}
🌟 Star Formation
:::
::: {style="color: #FFE42A; font-size: 1.1em;"}
Machine learning for astrophysics
:::

## What Are Young Stellar Objects? {#yso-intro}


- Protostars still in the process of forming
- Embedded in dense clouds of gas and dust
- Visible light is blocked — only detectable in **infrared**
- Studying YSOs reveals how stars like our Sun formed

## The MIRION "Yellowballs" Catalog

Named for their distinctive yellow-green appearance in Spitzer Space Telescope infrared images, Yellowballs are a population of compact YSO candidates.

> Can we predict their physical properties from their light alone?


## The Features {#yso-data}

**43 Features** from 4 observatories

- 10 infrared flux measurements spanning **8–1100 μm**
- Per-band photometric error and quality columns
- Sources: data comes from several differen sources

## The Targets {#yso-targets .smaller}


**8 Target Variables** to predict

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Distance-Independent**

| Target | Property |
|--------|----------|
| TEMP | Effective temperature |
| T_BOL | Bolometric temperature |
| LM | Luminosity/mass ratio |
| LRATIO | Luminosity ratio |
| SURF_DENS | Surface density |
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Distance-Dependent**

| Target | Property |
|--------|----------|
| L_BOL | Bolometric luminosity |
| MASS | Stellar mass |
| DIAM | Diameter |
:::

::::

## The Pipeline {#yso-pipeline}

For each of the **8 targets**, an independent pipeline was run:

::: {.incremental}
- **Bayesian optimization** (150 iterations): efficient hyperparameter search across a large space — smarter than grid search
- **6 model types searched simultaneously**: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting (Three different implementations), ElasticNet, MLP
- **Preprocessing pipeline**: imputating missing values → feature scaling → model fitting
- **Best single model** selected and saved per target
:::

## Running at Scale: Borah HPC {#yso-cluster .smaller}

Each target = **150 BayesSearchCV iterations × 5-fold CV** = 750 model fits

Submitted to Boise State/Idaho National Lab's Borah HPC cluster — one job per target.

| Resource | Per Job |
|----------|---------|
| CPUs | 48 cores |
| RAM | 128 GB |

## The Power of Parallelism {#yso-cluster-parallel}

All 8 targets ran simultaneously:

**48 CPUs × 8 jobs = 384 cores working in parallel**

::: {.fragment}
- What would take **a week on a laptop** runs **overnight on a cluster**
- Wall time: ~24 hrs total vs. ~192 hrs (8 days) sequentially
- HPC makes systematic hyperparameter search practical
:::

## Results: How Well Can We Predict? {#yso-results .smaller}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Distance-Independent**

| Description | CV R² |
|-------------|------:|
| Effective temperature | 0.916 |
| Luminosity/mass ratio | 0.890 |
| Surface density | 0.826 |
| Luminosity ratio | 0.474 |
| Bolometric temperature | 0.473 |
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Distance-Dependent**

| Description | CV R² |
|-------------|------:|
| Bolometric luminosity | 0.553 |
| Stellar mass | 0.427 |
| Diameter | 0.093 |
:::

::::

::: {.fragment}
**XGBoost was the best model for 7 of 8 targets** (GradientBoosting won for DIAM)
:::

## Temperature: R² = 0.916 {#yso-temp}

![](images/sf_temp_performance.png){.r-stretch fig-align="center"}


## Where to go from here... {#yso-lessons}

::: {.incremental}
- Try different transformations... (ratios of wavelengths are important)
- Regularization
- Story telling
- Bigger project: extracting wavelengths from images (this summer)
:::

---

[← Back to Topics](#toc)
